title: Leek-y Parmesan Chickpeas
summary: These creamy, cheesy chickpeas go great with just about everything. Serve them with fluffy rice or flatbreads for a full-on meal. We love quick chickpea recipes.

method.description: Wash, trim, and slice the leeks in half lengthways, then cut into thin half-moons. Thinly slice the garlic.
Heat a little olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the leeks and garlic, then cook for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft and jammy.
Stir in the Dijon mustard and chilli flakes, then cook for another minute.
Add the drained chickpeas and chicken stock, then bring to a simmer. Cook for 3–4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and becomes creamy.
Grate in the Parmesan and season to taste with salt, black pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Serve immediately.

tips: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.
To freeze, portion into containers and freeze for up to 3 months. Defrost in the fridge overnight and reheat in a pan or microwave.
This dish works well for meal prep—reheat portions throughout the week for a quick and nutritious meal.
Swap chickpeas for butter beans or cannellini beans.
Use vegetable stock instead of chicken stock for a vegetarian option.
Replace Parmesan with nutritional yeast for a dairy-free alternative.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
